message.content:
{"analysis":"I have enough understanding of the structure now. Rather than reverse-engineering every assembly detail (which is very tedious), I understand the overall algorithm well from the disassembly and image analysis:

- 800x600 image, camera at origin (0,0,0) looking down -z
- viewport: aspect 800/600, viewport_height related to 2.666667 (width) and 1.333333... wait 2.6667 is width, 1.3333 height. Actually those are likely viewport dimensions. -1.5 is the sphere center, the main sphere is at (0, 0, -1.5) maybe.
- sphere_intersect called with sphere center (0,-0.5,...) etc
- Plane at y=-0.5 with checkerboard (0.9 / 0.4)
- Light direction normalized (1,1,0.5)
- Sky gradient lerp white(1,1,1) to (0.5,0.7,1.0)

Let me look at the main.txt file content to get the exact sphere parameters and the gamma/shading.","plan":"1. View main.txt content for the sphere setup and shading
2. Write the C program","commands":[{"keystrokes":"sed -n '120,260p' /tmp/main.txt
","duration":0.1}]}
